title: impatiens xanthina var. xanthina
intro: Impatiens xanthina var. xanthina, commonly known as yellow balsam, is a flowering plant native to the Himalayas and China. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting respiratory and digestive health. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for medicinal purposes.
